CREATE TABLE time_series (ts TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO time_series SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-02', INTERVAL '1 HOUR');
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INTEGER, ts TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2001-01-02');
SELECT a.id, a.ts, b.ts FROM another_table a JOIN time_series b ON a.ts = b.ts;
SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01 01:30:00', INTERVAL '15 MINUTE') AS generated_ts;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-10', INTERVAL '1 DAY') AS gen_ts) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE events (event_time TIMESTAMP, event_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO events VALUES (TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', 'Event1'), (TIMESTAMP '2001-01-02', 'Event2');
SELECT e.event_name, g.gen_ts FROM events e CROSS JOIN (SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-05', INTERVAL '1 DAY') AS gen_ts) g WHERE e.event_time < g.gen_ts;
SELECT DISTINCT g.gen_ts FROM generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-03', INTERVAL '1 DAY') AS g WHERE g.gen_ts NOT IN (SELECT event_time FROM events);

SELECT g.gen_ts, COUNT(e.event_name) FROM generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-10', INTERVAL '1 DAY') AS g LEFT JOIN events e ON g.gen_ts = e.event_time GROUP BY g.gen_ts ORDER BY g.gen_ts;

SELECT g.ts AS generated_ts FROM (SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-05', INTERVAL '2 DAY') AS ts) g WHERE g.ts BETWEEN TIMESTAMP '2001-01-02' AND TIMESTAMP '2001-01-04';
CREATE TABLE test_join (start_time TIMESTAMP, end_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_join VALUES (TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-03'), (TIMESTAMP '2001-01-04', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-06');

SELECT j.start_time, j.end_time, g.gen_ts FROM test_join j, generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-06', INTERVAL '1 DAY') AS g(gen_ts) WHERE g.gen_ts BETWEEN j.start_time AND j.end_time;
SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2001-01-10', TIMESTAMP '2001-01-10 23:00:00', INTERVAL '1 HOUR') AS hourly_series;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (dt TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO complex_table SELECT generate_series(TIMESTAMP '2021-01-01', TIMESTAMP '2021-01-02', INTERVAL '30 MINUTE');
SELECT dt, dt + INTERVAL '1 HOUR' AS next_hour FROM complex_table WHERE dt > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '2 DAY';
